problem: When $p(x) = Ax^5 + Bx^3 + Cx + 4$ is divided by $x - 3,$ the remainder is 11.  Find the remainder when $p(x)$ is divided by $x + 3.$

classified_questions.question: What is the Remainder Theorem and how is it useful in finding remainders when dividing polynomials?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How does the fact that the remainder is 11 when $p(x)$ is divided by $x-3$ help us in solving this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the importance of finding $p(3)$ in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we use the information $p(3)=11$ to help us find the values of $A$, $B$ and $C$ in the polynomial $p(x)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why do we evaluate $p(-3)$ when trying to find the remainder of $p(x)$ when divided by $x+3$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the significance of the signs of the coefficients in the polynomial $p(x)$ when finding the remainder of $p(x)$ when divided by $x+3$?
classified_questions.label: f
